station.title: Student feedback layer
station.description: Student-facing root causes, recommendations, and feedback text.

metrics.adaptive_feedback:
=== ROOT CAUSES OF YOUR PERFORMANCE ===

Time Management (Priority: HIGH)
You appear to have written under time pressure. Start assignments early to allow time for revision and improvement.

=== FOR YOUR PROFILE (Engaged but Weak Writers - Good effort, needs support) ===
Focus on consistent effort and reflection on your writing.

=== SPECIFIC ACTIONS YOU SHOULD TAKE ===
1. RECOMMEND_TIME_MANAGEMENT: Encourage early start and distributed effort

=== KEY INSIGHT ===
Time pressure is affecting your writing quality.

=== RULE-BASED DIAGNOSTIC FEEDBACK ===
Excellent! Seeking assistance demonstrates awareness of your learning needs and a strong commitment to improvement.
Dialogic scaffolding

=== RECOMMENDED INTERVENTIONS ===
تقديم إثراء إضافي; جلسة حوار توضيحي; تشجيع وتعزيز السلوك الإيجابي
Clarification conference
مواد متقدمة وتحديات إضافية
تعزيز وإثراء
help_seeking_examples: Hii miss I received your message but i don't have assignment

While writing my paragraph, I had difficulty organizing my ideas clearly. I tried to solve this by adding an example about students using AI to summarize articles instead of understanding them. However, I am not sure if this example supports my argument well. Could you tell me if my example is clear enough or how I can improve it? Also something else I 'am not abut the structure if it is wright?

First, an important reason to limit the use of artificial intelligence in academic work is that it can weaken students’ critical thinking and writing skills. University education aims to help students analyze information and develop their own arguments. However, when students rely heavily on AI tools to generate answers or structure essays, they may practice these essential skills less frequently. For example, a student may use AI to summarize articles or write paragraphs instead of analyzing the ideas independently. According to UNESCO (2023), excessive dependence on AI writing tools can reduce students’ engagement in deep learning and independent problem-solving. Indeed, this shows that students may understand the topic less when AI performs the intellectual work for them. Therefore, their ability to think critically and build logical arguments can gradually decline. In conclusion, uncontrolled use of artificial intelligence may negatively affect students’ intellectual development and academic growth.
recommendations: RECOMMEND_TIME_MANAGEMENT: Encourage early start and distributed effort
